# Tab Bar 1.0.0

This file is derived from the exact Figlets component spec at `component-specs/Tab Bar 1.0.0.md`.
> A compact tab bar for switching between Text, File, and Stickies views while clearly indicating the active destination.
## Variants
| Dimension | Values | Implementation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Active | Text · File · Stickies | ComponentSet variant |

### Variant purposes

| Variant | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| Active=Text | Text view selected |
| Active=File | File view selected |
| Active=Stickies | Stickies view selected |

## Usage Rules
**Do:**
- Use it to switch between peer-level views within the same context.
- Keep tab labels short, clear, and consistent with their destination.
- Expose exactly one active tab at a time.

**Don't:**
- Do not use it as the application's primary navigation.
- Do not use tabs to trigger one-off actions.
- Do not show multiple tabs as active simultaneously.
## Accessibility
- Implement the container and items with appropriate tablist and tab semantics.
- Expose the selected tab programmatically and keep exactly one tab selected.
- Support keyboard navigation between tabs and activation using standard platform conventions.
- Preserve a clearly visible focus indicator for keyboard users.
- Maintain sufficient contrast for labels, active states, and focus indicators across supported modes.
- Keep interactive targets large enough for comfortable touch and pointer use.
- Use design tokens for spacing, color, typography, and radius; the current raw 5px component-set radius remains an outstanding binding decision.
